title: MD
biography: Virginia Apgar was an American physician, anesthesiologist, and medical researcher who developed the Apgar Score in 1952, a simple and rapid method for assessing the health of newborn infants that remains in universal use today. She was the first woman to be appointed a full professor at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons and later became a leading advocate for birth defects research at the March of Dimes.
aiSummary: Virginia Apgar was an anesthesiology and neonatology specialist with an H-index of 25 at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons. Developed the Apgar Score (1952) for rapid newborn health assessment, now used universally worldwide. Based in United States.
techniquesInvented: Apgar Score for newborn infant assessment
